frameworks.name: auditory steady-state response (ASSR) biomarker
frameworks.one_line: 40 Hz ASSR as oscillatory readout of cortical synaptic interactions

frameworks.name: E/I balance pharmacology
frameworks.one_line: dissociating NMDA excitation vs GABA-A inhibition contributions to gamma response

core_question: Does the 40 Hz auditory steady-state response in human auditory cortex depend on NMDA excitation or GABA-A inhibition?
key_assumptions: 40 Hz ASSR reflects synaptic interactions in cortical circuits
low-dose lorazepam selectively probes GABA-A inhibition
low-dose memantine selectively probes NMDA receptor function
MEG recovers auditory cortical gamma in healthy participants
manipulations: pharmacological agent (placebo vs lorazepam vs memantine)
key_findings: robust 40 Hz ASSR present under placebo, especially right hemisphere
lorazepam (GABA-A agonist) increased 40 Hz ASSR amplitude
memantine (NMDA blocker) had no detectable effect on the response
interpretations: 40 Hz ASSR in auditory cortex is shaped by GABA-A-mediated intracortical inhibition
ASSR biomarker tracks inhibitory, not NMDA-glutamatergic, synaptic function
